title: 10 Herbal Essential Oils For Hypothyroidism
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils can be a natural way to help manage hypothyroidism, a condition where the thyroid gland doesn't produce enough hormones. One reason herbal teas can be beneficial is that they can stimulate the thyroid gland to produce more hormones, improving metabolism and energy levels. This can lead to a range of benefits, including weight loss, improved mood, and increased motivation.  For example, Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, has been traditionally used to treat hypothyroidism. Its active compounds can help stimulate the thyroid gland, increasing hormone production and improving overall health. Another herb, Cassia auriculata, also known as Siris, is believed to have adaptogenic properties that can help the body adapt to stress and balance hormone production. Withania somnifera, or ashwagandha, is another herb that can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common symptoms of hypothyroidism.  Drinking herbal teas made from these herbs can be a simple and natural way to incorporate them into your daily routine. You can add them to your diet as a warm drink, or use them as a base for other herbal remedies. Some people also use essential oils extracted from these herbs, which can be applied topically or inhaled to help stimulate the thyroid gland and promote hormone balance. However, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies, especially if you're already taking medication for hypothyroidism.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils contains triterpenoid saponins, including glycyrrhetinic acid, which is known to mimic the effects of cortisol in the body. Cortisol is a hormone that helps regulate metabolism, including the metabolism of thyroid hormones. Glycyrrhetinic acid also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland that can contribute to hypothyroidism. Additionally, glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils contains flavonoids, such as luteolin and quercetin, which have antioxidant properties that can help protect the thyroid gland from oxidative stress. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils may help support thyroid function and alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal essential oils for hypothyroidism
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots. Clean and chop them into small pieces.
Place 2 cups of water in a saucepan and add the chopped roots. Bring to a boil then reduce heat.
Simmer for 2 hours. Strain the mixture into a bowl using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Combine the strained liquid with 1 cup of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil) in a separate bowl.
Let the mixture sit for 24 hours. Strain it again and discard the roots. Your essential oil is now ready for use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cassia auriculata essential oils contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids and phenolic acids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland. The flavonoids in Cassia auriculata, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, have been shown to increase the production of thyroid hormones by stimulating the thyroid gland's function. The phenolic acids, such as ferulic acid, can also help protect the thyroid gland from oxidative stress and damage. By reducing inflammation and promoting thyroid hormone production, Cassia auriculata essential oils may be beneficial in supporting the treatment of hypothyroidism.
remedies.image_alt: Cassia auriculata herbal essential oils for hypothyroidism
remedy_procedure: Gather 250 grams of Cassia auriculata leaves and flowers. Clean and dry them.
Use a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil (1 cup) and mix it with the Cassia auriculata leaves and flowers in a glass container.
Steep the mixture in a warm place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the container daily. This allows the oil to extract from the plant.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container. Discard the solids.
Store the extracted Cassia auriculata essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight lid. Use 5-7 drops of the oil in a carrier oil for massage or topical application for hypothyroidism relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Withania somnifera essential oils contains a range of bioactive constituents, including withanolides, alkaloids, and saponins. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hypothyroidism. The withanolides in Withania somnifera essential oils have been shown to increase thyroid hormone production and improve thyroid function in animal studies. Additionally, the alkaloids present in this essential oil have been found to have a regulating effect on the thyroid gland, helping to normalize thyroid hormone levels. The antioxidant properties of Withania somnifera essential oils may also help to protect the thyroid gland from damage caused by free radicals.
remedies.image_alt: Withania somnifera herbal essential oils for hypothyroidism
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Withania somnifera roots and 1 cup of carrier oil like coconut or olive oil.
Grind the dried Withania somnifera roots into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the ground roots with 8 ounces of carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to help the oil extract.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng essential oils contains bioactive constituents like ginsenosides, which are known to stimulate the production of thyroid hormones. These hormones play a crucial role in regulating metabolism, energy levels, and weight management, all of which can be affected by hypothyroidism. The ginsenosides in Panax ginseng essential oils also have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the thyroid gland. Additionally, the oils contain compounds like panaxynol and ginsenol, which have been shown to increase the activity of thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH) and improve thyroid function. By stimulating the thyroid gland and reducing oxidative stress, Panax ginseng essential oils may help alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism, such as fatigue, weight gain, and cold intolerance.
remedies.image_alt: Panax ginseng herbal essential oils for hypothyroidism
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Panax ginseng root and a clean glass jar.
Add 2 cups of carrier oil (like jojoba or sweet almond oil) to the jar.
Place the dried Panax ginseng root in a cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Steep the root in the carrier oil for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the oil and discard the root. Store the Panax ginseng essential oil in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus essential oils contains bioactive constituents like astragaloside IV, flavonoids, and saponins. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce thyroid inflammation and oxidative stress. The anti-inflammatory properties of astragaloside IV can also help decrease the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines that can contribute to hypothyroidism. Additionally, the saponins in Astragalus membranaceus essential oils can enhance the production of thyroid hormones and improve thyroid function. The antioxidant properties of flavonoids can also help protect the thyroid gland from damage caused by free radicals.
remedies.image_alt: Astragalus membranaceus herbal essential oils for hypothyroidism
remedy_procedure: Gather 100g of Astragalus membranaceus root. Dry and clean the root.
Combine the root with 1 cup of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil) in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a warm, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid. Use 5-7 drops per day for hypothyroidism relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils contains rosmarinic acid, camphor, and bornyl acetate, which are potent antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. These bioactive constituents help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, common triggers for hypothyroidism. The anti-inflammatory properties in rosmary essential oil also help alleviate thyroid-related inflammation and promote healthy thyroid function. Additionally, rosmarinic acid has been shown to improve circulation and promote the removal of toxins from the thyroid gland, supporting its proper functioning. By using Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils, some people have reported improvements in their thyroid health, including increased metabolism and energy.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal essential oils for hypothyroidism
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and stems. Dry the leaves and stems for 1-2 weeks, or until they are crumbly.
Add 2 tablespoons of carrier oil (like coconut or jojoba oil) to a small saucepan. Warm the oil over low heat.
Add 2 tablespoons of the dried Rosmarinus officinalis to the warmed oil. Stir well.
Let the mixture sit for 2-3 weeks, shaking the saucepan daily. This will help the essential oil infuse into the carrier oil.
Strain the oil into a clean container through a coffee filter or cheesecloth. Discard the solids and label the essential oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum essential oils contains a group of bioactive constituents called flavonoids, lignans, and sesquiterpenes. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body. Flavonoids, such as silymarin, have been shown to improve thyroid function by increasing the production of thyroid hormones. Lignans, like sesamin, have been found to increase the activity of enzymes that help to detoxify the thyroid gland. By reducing inflammation and promoting detoxification, Silybum marianum essential oils may help to alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism.
remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal essential oils for hypothyroidism
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Silybum marianum seeds. Clean and dry them thoroughly.
In a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over boiling water, add 2 cups of carrier oil like coconut or olive oil.
Add 1/2 cup of Silybum marianum seeds to the carrier oil. Stir well.
Heat the mixture for 4-6 hours, stirring occasionally. Then, let it cool.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle. Discard the seeds.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa essential oils contains bioactive constituents like curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin, which have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, which is often associated with hypothyroidism. Curcumin also has a positive effect on the enzyme 5'-deiodinase, which is necessary for converting T4 into the more active form of thyroid hormone, T3. Furthermore, curcuma longa essential oils has been shown to improve thyroid function by increasing the levels of thyroid hormones in the body. By reducing inflammation and improving thyroid hormone production, curcuma longa essential oils may be beneficial for individuals with hypothyroidism.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal essential oils for hypothyroidism
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Curcuma longa roots. Clean and dry them.
Fill a saucepan with 2 cups of water. Bring the water to a boil.
Reduce heat and add 1/2 cup of Curcuma longa roots to the water. Let it simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the solids. Collect the liquid in a separate container.
Use a still or let the liquid sit in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks to allow the essential oil to separate and rise to the top.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils contains a compound called gingerol, which is known for its anti-inflammatory properties. Gingerol helps to reduce swelling and pain in the thyroid gland, making it easier for the thyroid to function properly. Zingiber officinale essential oils also contains a compound called shogaol, which has been shown to stimulate the thyroid gland and increase the production of thyroid hormones. The warming and stimulating properties of Zingiber officinale essential oils can help to improve circulation and increase the delivery of oxygen and nutrients to the thyroid gland. This can help to support the thyroid gland and alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism, such as fatigue and weight gain.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for hypothyroidism
remedy_procedure: Get 2 cups of fresh Zingiber officinale roots. Wash them clean and chop into small pieces.
Combine 1 cup of chopped roots with 4 cups of water in a saucepan. Heat on low until roots start to soften.
Remove saucepan from heat and let it sit for 2 hours. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl.
Add 1 tablespoon of carrier oil (like jojoba oil) to the strained mixture. Stir well.
Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle. Use 5-7 drops per day, mixed with a carrier oil, for hypothyroidism relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils contains linalool and linalyl acetate, which are known for their ability to reduce stress and promote relaxation. These properties can help alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism, such as anxiety and fatigue, by regulating the body's hormonal balance. The essential oils also contain borneol and camphor, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the thyroid gland and improve its function. Additionally, the oil's antioxidant properties can help protect the thyroid gland from damage caused by free radicals, promoting a healthy thyroid function. By reducing stress and inflammation, Lavandula angustifolia essential oils may help support the body's natural thyroid function and alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils for hypothyroidism
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Lavandula angustifolia flowers and 2 cups of water in a large pot.
Heat the water in the pot over low heat and let it simmer for 1 hour.
Add the Lavandula angustifolia flowers to a glass jar and pour the cooled water over them.
Steep the flowers in the water for 2 hours, then strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a separate container.
Store the resulting liquid in a dark glass bottle and let it sit for 7-10 days to allow the essential oils to separate and rise to the top.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent hypothyroidism in some cases. Certain herbs like ashwagandha and bladderwrack are believed to support thyroid health by stimulating hormone production and reducing inflammation. However, more research is needed to confirm these effects. Some people find that drinking these teas regularly helps maintain their thyroid function.
faq_safe: Consuming herbal teas for hypothyroidism daily can be safe, but it's essential to check the ingredients. Some teas, like ashwagandha and ginseng, may interact with medications or worsen thyroid issues. Others, like chamomile, are generally considered safe. Moderate amounts are key, as excessive consumption can lead to side effects.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with hypothyroidism symptoms, but it takes time to see results. Some people notice improvements in a few weeks, while others may not see a difference for 2-3 months. It's essential to drink herbal teas consistently and combine them with a balanced diet to support thyroid health.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for hypothyroidism can be beneficial in the morning. This is because the herbs can help stimulate digestion and boost metabolism, which can help regulate thyroid function. A morning cup can give you a natural energy boost to start your day.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for hypothyroidism. Some herbs, like kelp and bladderwrack, contain iodine which can increase thyroid hormone levels. This may affect how medications like levothyroxine work. If you're taking these medications, it's best to limit or avoid herbal teas that contain iodine to avoid any potential problems.
faq_pregnant: While some herbal teas may help with hypothyroidism symptoms, pregnant women should be cautious. Certain herbs like kelp, bladderwrack, and dandelion root can affect thyroid function and interact with medications. However, others like peppermint, chamomile, and raspberry leaf are often considered safe during pregnancy. It's best to choose teas with a known track record and start with small amounts.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas for hypothyroidism can be a good option, but be careful when breastfeeding. Some herbs like kelp and bladderwrack may interfere with thyroid medication and cause too much thyroid hormone. This could harm your baby. It's best to choose teas with gentle herbs like peppermint or chamomile that are safe for breastfeeding moms.
title_2: 10 Herbal Essential Oils for Women to Combat Hypothyroidism
